name: kmonitor

metadata.name: kmonitor
metadata.summary: kmonitor is a simple utility wrapper around kubernetes package

documentation:
# K8s Monitor 

## Description

Simple wrapper around `kubernetes` package to monitor the status of a k8s deployment.

## Installation

```bash
pip install kmonitor --upgrade
```

## Usage

```python
import numpy as np
from kmonitor import KubeMonitor, safe_jsonify


if __name__ == '__main__':
  km = KubeMonitor()
  pods = km.list_pods()
  if pods is not None:
    for pod in pods:
      print(f"Pod: {pod.metadata.name} in namespace {pod.metadata.namespace} is in phase {pod.status.phase}")
  else:
    print("Failed to get pods")

  namespaces = km.list_namespaces()
  if namespaces is not None:
    for ns in namespaces:
      print(f"Namespace: {ns.metadata.name}")
  else:
    print("Failed to get namespaces")
  
  nr_pods = len(pods)
  idx = np.random.randint(0, nr_pods)
  example_pod_name = "basic-test" # pods[idx].metadata.name
  print("Checking pod status for pod {}".format(example_pod_name))
  status = km.check_pod_by_name(example_pod_name)
  print(safe_jsonify(status, indent=2))

  example_pod_names = ["nvidia", "calico-node", "emqx"]
  print("Checking pod status for pods {}".format(example_pod_names))
  status = km.check_pods_by_names(example_pod_names)
  print(safe_jsonify(status, indent=2))
```

## Citation

If you use this code in your research, please consider citing it with the following BibTeX entry:

```bibtex
@misc{KMonitor,
  author = {Andrei Damian},
  title = {kmonitor - a simple K8s monitor framework},
  year = {2023-2024},
  publisher = {GitHub},
  journal = {https://github.com/aidamian/k8s_monitor.git}
}
```

methods.name: kmonitor.mixins.nodes_mixin._NodesMixin.get_all_nodes
documentation:
Retrieves all nodes in the cluster along with their status.

Returns
-------
list
    A list of nodes and their statuses.

methods.name: kmonitor.mixins.nodes_mixin._NodesMixin.get_nodes_metrics
documentation:
Fetches metrics for all nodes and converts them to readable units.

Returns
-------
list
    A list of nodes with their CPU (in millicores) and memory usage (in GiB).

methods.name: kmonitor.mixins.pods_mixin._PodsMixin.check_pod
documentation:
Check the health of a pod by its name and namespace.

Parameters
----------
namespace : str
    The namespace where the pod is located.
pod_name : str
    The name of the pod to check.

Returns
-------
  dict
    The health status of the pod.

methods.name: kmonitor.mixins.pods_mixin._PodsMixin.delete_pods_from_namespace
documentation:
Delete all pods in a namespace that start with a specific name.

Parameters
----------
base_name : str
    The base name of the pods to delete.
namespace : str
    The namespace where the pods are located.
Mixin class for pod-related operations. It assumes the `self.api` is initialized by the main class.
